title: 20 Things Every New Cheer Mom Wishes She'd Known
description: Crowdsourced from 1,200+ moms in our reader survey: the 20 biggest lessons about costs, gym culture, competition day, and what "optional" really means.
ogDescription: The monthly tuition is a third of your real spend. "Optional" almost always means expected. Pack two pairs of every white sock. 20 lessons, starting hot.
headline: things every new *cheer mom* wishes she'd known on day one.
sub: Crowdsourced from 1,200+ moms in our reader survey. The hot ones first.

items.title: The monthly tuition is a third of your real spend.
items.body: If your gym quotes $295/mo, plan for ~$24K total. The other two-thirds arrive as separate invoices.

items.title: "Optional" almost always means "expected."
items.body: Privates, team mom fund, banquet, photo package. Read the room.

items.title: Tour the gym during a Level 5 practice.
items.body: The lobby is theater. The mat during senior practice is the real culture.

items.title: Pack two pairs of every white sock.
items.body: You will lose one. You will need the other on Sunday morning. Trust me.

items.title: Buy the gym's bow. Twice.
items.body: You will lose one, and you cannot show up Day Two without the matching bow.

items.title: Book hotels through the team block — but the night BEFORE.
items.body: Day-before flights save $400 and your sanity. Block rates extend by request.

items.title: Comp days are 14 hours. Pack snacks for you.
items.body: The vending machine at 11pm in a convention center is a $9 granola bar.

items.title: The coach you sign up for is not always the coach who shows.
items.body: Ask about choreo turnover. Some gyms cycle 'guest' choreographers — find out who's actually on the mat.

items.title: The group chat is a job.
items.body: Mute it on weekends. Reply with one emoji. The world will keep spinning.

items.title: Privates aren't a 'next step' — they're an industry.
items.body: Most gyms make 40% of profit on private lessons. Treat them as optional even when the coach says they aren't.

items.title: Your kid will quit at least once.
items.body: Don't panic. Wait 72 hours. They almost always come back.

items.title: There is a "good gym, bad gym" in every metro.
items.body: Ask alumni — the gym's reputation among ex-athletes is the real signal.

items.title: Get the season total IN WRITING before signing.
items.body: Verbal "around $5K" quotes turn into $14K invoices by November.

items.title: The "competition pony" is a $40 add-on at most comps.
items.body: YouTube it once, do it yourself thereafter.

items.title: Score sheets are public. Read them.
items.body: After every comp, the score sheets explain exactly where you lost points. Most parents never look.

items.title: Your kid's body changes. So will the team.
items.body: Late puberty growth spurts often move flyers to bases. This is normal. The gym should be honest about it.

items.title: Asking what something costs is not rude.
items.body: Ever. The gym is a business. Treat it like one.

items.title: Sundays are about being on time, not being early.
items.body: 2-hour pre-call is gym tradition, not gym requirement. Save your kid's legs for the floor.

items.title: Don't post the routine video before it's legal to.
items.body: Most gyms have a no-post window for choreo IP. Violating it can mean dropped placement.

items.title: Cheer ends. Your kid is more than this.
items.body: 18 is the cliff for most. Build her identity outside the gym while she's still in it.

blocks.title: What's *your* #21?
blocks.body: We update this list every quarter from reader submissions. Send yours — we'll credit you if we publish it.
